operational_knowledge_units.title: Tipper tag model in Sorter Sweeper Database
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide describes a tag model where each tipper has 12 subscribed tags: 7 input tags and 5 output tags. OptiSweep Software receives messages from the tipper on output tags and writes to input tags.
observed_evidence: Slide title: 'Sorter Sweeper Database / Tag Model'
Text states: 'Every tipper has 12 tags that we subscribe to. 7 in and 5 out.'
Text states: 'OPTISweep Software receives messages from the tipper on the out Tags and write to the In Tags.'
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Sorter Sweeper Database tag model reference. Each tipper has 12 subscribed tags, with 7 in and 5 out. OptiSweep Software receives messages from the tipper on out tags and writes to in tags.

operational_knowledge_units.title: Tag model field definitions
operational_knowledge_units.summary: The slide defines four tag model fields: TagType defines the tag and is used to process the action; Bit indicates whether a received tag should be broken into bits; Value shows the current value; Flag tracks when the tipper went into cycle.
observed_evidence: Bullet: 'TagType: Defines the Tag and is used to process the action.'
Bullet: 'Bit: indicates if that tag should be broken up into bits when received'
Bullet: 'Flag: Is used to track when the tipper went into cycle.'

relationships.meaning: Defines the tag and is used to process the action
Indicates if the tag should be broken into bits when received
Tracks when the tipper went into cycle

operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: Tag model glossary definitions for Sorter Sweeper Database. TagType defines the tag and is used to process the action. Bit indicates whether a tag should be broken into bits when received. Value shows the current value. Flag tracks when the tipper went into cycle.

operational_knowledge_units.title: OptiSweep tag communication behavior with tipper
operational_knowledge_units.summary: Operationally, the system behavior shown is asymmetric: OptiSweep consumes messages from tipper out tags and sends values by writing to tipper in tags.
observed_evidence: Text states: 'OPTISweep Software receives messages from the tipper on the out Tags and write to the In Tags.'
Text states there are separate in and out tags for each tipper
operational_knowledge_units.retrieval_text: System behavior for OptiSweep and tipper tags: OptiSweep receives messages from the tipper on out tags and writes to in tags. The tag model separates communication into 7 in tags and 5 out tags per tipper.

Creating operational steps from this slide would require inference beyond the slide evidence.
procedure_candidate_attempts.retrieval_text: Discarded as a procedure candidate because the slide is a tag model/database reference slide. It defines tag counts, communication direction, and field meanings, but contains no explicit reusable action guidance or ordered steps.
